master.intent: quote-first product ad with premium proof support
notes: Quote card is the hero persuasion block.
Proof bar sits close to CTA or product meta.

1:1._layout_note: Square split-panel: 4:5-source image LEFT (full-height half), dark content panel RIGHT. Mirrors the landscape design language (display_script headline + eyebrow_rules + with_verified_buyers proof_bar + with_author_photo quote_card + section_header + body_description + callouts badge_row + cta).

4:5._layout_note: Portrait stacked split-panel: 4:5-source image on TOP (full-width 1000×625), dark content panel on BOTTOM (1000×625). Same design language as landscape — display_script headline + eyebrow_rules + with_verified_buyers proof_bar + with_author_photo quote_card + section_header + body_description + callouts badge_row + cta — laid out wider but shorter than 16:9.

9:16._layout_note: Stories split-panel: 4:5-source image on TOP (1000×889), dark content panel BOTTOM (1000×889). Headline + eyebrow_rules span full panel width; below the eyebrow the panel splits into a 60/40 two-column layout — LEFT (proof_bar / quote_card / section_header / product_meta) reads as the testimonial column, RIGHT (badge_row top, cta bottom) reads as the proof-and-action column.

16:9._layout_note: Landscape split-panel layout: full-bleed source media on the left 40%, dark content panel on the right 60% with stacked elements (headline / eyebrow_rules / proof_bar / quote_card / section_header / body / badge_row / cta).
16:9._zone_scalers_note: Per-zone-id multipliers applied at render time to slot height + font-size (and any em-relative descendants). The slot budget calculator reads the same map so Gemini's max_chars for the eyebrow reflects the wider rendered glyphs. Adjust per ratio when the column visually under- or over-weights against the reference creative.

1.91:1._layout_note: Landscape split-panel layout — sibling of 16:9 with tighter vertical budget (524h vs 563h).

master.intent: image-led split-panel product/brand ad — shares the testimonial_spotlight design language but swaps the quote_card slot for section_header + product_meta (description-led instead of testimonial-led)
notes: Image-led split-panel — same geometry as testimonial_spotlight.
section_header + product_meta replace the quote_card slot for product/brand description instead of customer quote.
4:5 source crop is the hero (Cloudinary c_fill,g_auto smart-crops to fit each ratio's image rect).

1:1._layout_note: Square split-panel: 4:5-source image LEFT (full-height half), dark content panel RIGHT. Mirrors testimonial_spotlight 1:1 with section_header + product_meta replacing the quote_card slot.

4:5._layout_note: Portrait stacked split-panel: 4:5-source image on TOP (full-width 1000×625), dark content panel on BOTTOM (1000×625). Mirrors testimonial_spotlight 4:5 with section_header + product_meta replacing the quote_card slot.

9:16._layout_note: Stories split-panel: 4:5-source image on TOP (1000×889), dark content panel BOTTOM (1000×889). Mirrors testimonial_spotlight 9:16 with section_header + product_meta replacing the quote_card slot in the LEFT column.

16:9._layout_note: Landscape split-panel: 4:5-source image LEFT 40%, dark content panel RIGHT 60%. Mirrors testimonial_spotlight 16:9 with section_header + product_meta replacing the quote_card slot.

1.91:1._layout_note: Landscape split-panel — sibling of 16:9 with tighter vertical budget. Mirrors testimonial_spotlight 1.91:1 with section_header + product_meta replacing the quote_card slot.

master.intent: product-centered review density with multiple supporting review cards
notes: Review cards surround or flank hero.
Cards stay static even when hero is video.

master.intent: metric-led proof system with structured hierarchy and optional supportive media
notes: Metrics are the hero proof layer.
Support media is optional but helpful.
CTA should appear after numeric trust is established.

master.intent: creator-led recommendation with product support and trust ribbon
notes: Product sits as support or inset.
Trust ribbon should sit near handle, badges, or CTA.
